name: deny-loop-repeat
doc: Mechanical anti-loop (NEVER propose the same fix twice), GENUINE-retry path. Two IDENTICAL denied tool-calls spaced BEYOND the burst-dedup window (delayMs 2100 > BURST_DEDUP_MS 2000, src/runtime/burst-window.ts): the 2nd is a real retry, so it gets the message rewritten with a `[REPEAT]` title, a `STOP:` prefix and a forced research-expert action (enrichRepeatDeny, deny-loop.ts:65-74) while the DECISION stays block. A tiny `git reset --hard` deny (evaluate GIT_BLOCKED → block "Destructive git command") is the stable identical block. The sidecar deny-loop.json lives in dirname(trackFile) = the per-project state dir, persisting across the separate binary spawns (needs HOME+cwd stable). NB: two IDENTICAL denies fired WITHOUT the delay are folded into one by the fan-out dedup — that collapse is proved by 16-deny-loop-burst-dedup.json.

steps.doc: First deny is NORMAL (count 1, isRepeat false, deny-loop.ts:50-55). Reason = `[BLOCKED] Destructive git command`.

Identical retry, spaced past the burst window → count 2, isRepeat true → enrichRepeatDeny: title prefixed `[REPEAT]`, reason carries `Identical attempt #2` + `STOP`. Proves a GENUINE retry still escalates (the dedup only folds same-event siblings, not real retries).
